data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me create the Central Package Management setup:

Packages across all projects:
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting: 1.15.0 (Web)
- Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi: 8.0.24 (Web)
- System.Text.Json: 9.0.0 (Core)
- Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk: 17.12.0 (Tests)
- xunit: 2.9.3 (Tests)
- xunit.runner.visualstudio: 2.8.2 (Tests)


Since there are no version conflicts, I'll create a Directory.Packages.props file at the solution root with centralized version management enabled and all package versions defined, then strip the version attributes from each project's package references while preserving other attributes.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Done! Here's what I did:

**Created `Directory.Packages.props`** at the solution root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and all 6 package versions centralized (alphabetically ordered).

**Updated 3 .csproj files** — removed `Version` attributes from all `PackageReference` elements while preserving other attributes (e.g., `PrivateAssets="all"` on `xunit.runner.visualstudio`).

No version conflicts existed in this solution — each package was referenced by only one project.
